content.text: Yield Assessment desk. The corpse is inventory. The clock was running before it hit the ground.

One Check, one table, three clocks. On a kill, or at a Cache node (▣ — see Missions & the Spire Map), a carver makes the Harvesting Check vs a DC set by the creature's tier. Harvesting rolls Grit (the skill — see The Twelve Skills). Outcomes use the universal bands:
Full — clean yield: full grade, intact specials (glands, nodes, graft-viable organs).
Partial — yield at reduced grade, or full grade at a cost: time on the mission clock, or Saturation exposure — Bloom material is alive and doesn't consent.
Fail — ruined goods, or the hazard version of the partial costs.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: The creature-tier → DC mapping is unprinted. The only printed calibration point: graft-viable harvesting is a Hard 11 baseline (a Harvesting skill feature lowers it to Standard — see The Twelve Skills). Everything else on the DC ladder awaits a ruling.
The same taxonomy that picked your weapon now prices the corpse — intel, doctrine, and yield are one table read three ways (see Enemy Framework; every Bestiary block prints its own Carve line).

rows: Hide armors enter the crafting table — armor from the taxonomy that defines armor (see Armor, The Material Cost Law)
Tinkerer supply. How you kill it prices what it leaves
The wild-graft crafting feed (installs — see Mutations & Grafts). Carver / Street Doc / Wrangler crossover

content.text: Graft-viable organs are priced like the contraband they legally are. Declared, they sell at schedule rates; undeclared, they sell better and cost more. This is the premium tier of the trade, and the gate inspectors know it too.

CUSTOMS BULLETIN: Biological salvage of graft-viability class must be declared at the checkpoint. Declared organs are purchased at schedule rates. Undeclared organs are also purchased at schedule rates, eventually, by somebody.
Mission time. Carving isn't free at a hot node — every carve spends the same clock the contract runs on. A crew kneeling over a corpse is a crew not moving.
Preservation. Value halves after a window that varies by material type — fresh organs are minutes. This is the banking pressure made physical: the longer goods travel, the less they're worth.
Capacity. A Bloomtusk doesn't fit in a backpack. Carving creates cargo, cargo needs Capacity, and Capacity is a vehicle stat — see Vehicles.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Preservation windows by material type are unprinted. The halving rule and the "organs are minutes" anchor are the only printed points on the clock.

Carving creates cargo → cargo needs the van → the van needs the Rigger. The Carver profession raises grades, slows preservation decay, and owns the buyer network that turns goods into the 800-cr version of the 200-cr corpse — the perks live on Carver; the wheels live on Rigger.

content.label: TABLE NOTE
content.text: The preservation clock pulls crews toward corpses that are still warm. Treat a carve at a hot node as part of the encounter, not as cleanup after it — harvest exposure compounds like every other exposure in this game.
